answer:
The provided documents don't contain specific information about Microsoft's cloud segment growth year over year. 

While the documents mention that Microsoft's "Productivity and Business Processes" segment revenues were $77.5 billion in fiscal 2024, compared to $69.3 billion in fiscal 2023, an increase of 12% [Source 1], and that "Microsoft 365 Commercial cloud revenue grew 15% year-over-year" [Source 1], these refer to specific products within segments rather than the overall cloud segment performance.

The documents reference Microsoft's "Intelligent Cloud" as one of their three segments [Source 2], but don't provide the specific year-over-year growth figures for that segment.

PRODUCTIVITY AND BUSINESS PROCESSES

This segment includes Microsoft 365 (formerly Office 365), Exchange, SharePoint, Microsoft Teams, LinkedIn, and Dynamics 365. Segment revenues were $77.5 billion in fiscal 2024, compared to $69.3 billion in fiscal 2023, an increase of 12%.

Microsoft 365 Commercial cloud revenue grew 15% year-over-year, driven by continued expansion into enterprise customers and the integration of Copilot AI capabilities. Microsoft Corporation 10-K Annual Report — Fiscal Year 2024
United States Securities and Exchange Commission
Washington, D.C. 20549

ITEM 1. BUSINESS

Microsoft Corporation enables digital transformation for the era of an intelligent cloud and an intelligent edge. Microsoft develops and supports a wide range of software products, services, devices, and solutions. The Company's fiscal year ends June 30.

Microsoft's segments are: Productivity and Business Processes, Intelligent Cloud, and More Personal Computing.

The Company delivered approximately 1,789,226 vehicles in fiscal year 2024, compared to 1,808,581 deliveries in fiscal year 2023.

ENERGY GENERATION AND STORAGE SEGMENT

ble energy. To achieve this mission, the Company has designed and deployed a vertically integrated energy and transportation ecosystem.

AUTOMOTIVE SEGMENT

The Company's automotive segment includes the design, development, manufacturing, leasing, and sale of electric vehicles. The Company currently manufactures the Model 3, Model Y, Model S, Model X, and Cybertruck. Model Y is the Company's highest volume vehicle.

Capital expenditures for fiscal 2024 were $55.7 billion, primarily for cloud and AI infrastructure including data center construction, servers, and networking equipment. The Company expects to continue significant capital investment to support AI and cloud growth.
